General.name: Containment Level Engineering Type
General.summary: When modeling data where each row is strictly contained within the time range of a parent, this engineering type is used to indicate the depth of containment (i.e. the number of enclosing "parents".)
General.description: When modeling data where each row is strictly contained within the time range of a parent, this engineering type is used to indicate the depth of containment (i.e. the number of enclosing "parents".)

usage.identifier: Value is treated as an identifier.

Display Conventions.text: When a plot uses this type as a qualifier, then the treatment will reflect a containment hierarchy. This means that rows with a value of n will visual enclose rows with a value greater than n. The display does not enforce that contained row must fit within its parent's time spans. The modeler must ensure this is true to create a sensible visual presentation.

Encoding Notes.text: The root of the containment hierarchy is 0. If a row of data has 2 logical parents, then the value should be 2. The values need to be compact and skipping values leaves gaps in the layout.

General.name: Narrative Engineering Type
General.summary: An entry in narrative.
General.description: An entry in narrative.

usage.categorical: Value cannot be summed or averaged.  They have no inherent height in a graph.
usage.narrative: Value is a typed array, but displayed on a single line as a string.

Display Conventions.text: These are typically recursively displayed, where each piece of the narrative entry is formatted, and the narrative entry may contain as a piece, another narrative entry.

Encoding Notes.text: Typically a narrative can be thought of as a tables of these objects, occurring in chronological order, that tell a story about how something has evolved, like the life span of a thread, or how a scene is being constructed in a graphics pipeline.

General.name: Narrative Certainty Engineering Type
General.summary: A narrative entry certainty is a value from 1 to 100 that describes how certain a related narrative entry is.
General.description: A narrative entry certainty is a value from 1 to 100 that describes how certain a related narrative entry is.

usage.categorical: Value cannot be summed or averaged.  They have no inherent height in a graph.

Display Conventions.text: Narrative certainties can be given any number of visual treatments, but typically are not displayed as column values. They may be rendered as a different text color or background if they are uncertain, say below 50. Note: This engineering type is usually only displayed for debugging.  It's not intended to be displayed in an instrument or augmentation.

Encoding Notes.text: Higher numbers indicate higher certainty, where 100 is absolutely certain, and 0 is demonstrably questionable.

General.name: Narrative Significance Engineering Type
General.summary: A narrative entry significance is an enumerated value that describes how important a related narrative entry is.
General.description: A narrative entry significance is an enumerated value that describes how important a related narrative entry is.

usage.categorical: Value cannot be summed or averaged.  They have no inherent height in a graph.

Display Conventions.text: Narrative significance can be given any number of visual treatments, but typically are not displayed as column values. They may be filterable and treated with different colors, or simply collapsed if there is a run of low significant narratives. Note: This engineering type is usually only displayed for debugging.  It's not intended to be displayed in an instrument or augmentation.

Encoding Notes.text: They range from "pedantic", which means that the narrative entry is probably just repeating something that's already known, to "universal", which is something so significant that everyone would find it useful to know.

Display Conventions.text: Sessions are displayed using the description given by the developer (if present) and their internal id. Other attributes (e.g. whether a session is shared or ephemeral) may be included as well.

Encoding Notes.text: A combination of the internal session id, the given developer description and additional metadata. The first slot holds the session uuid, the second slot the developer specified background identifier (only for background sessions) the third slot holds the user-readable name of the session either specified by the developer (`session.sessionDescription`) or generated by the modeler. the fourth slot encodes a session type enum as a string, marking e.g. whether this session is the "shared" session or is an "ephemeral" session. The last and final slot contains a json-encoded dictionary with additional metadata about the session for additional properties to enable extensibility in the future. It's currently unused.
